candidates.name: Docusaurus and @docusaurus/plugin-content-docs

evidence.claim: The npm registry reported 3.10.2, MIT, Node >=20, modified 2026-07-10. The project already proves pinned 3.10.1 builds.
The official plugin supports a docs path relative to the site directory and configurable sidebar ownership.

risks: The external parent-directory path must be proven in the clean CI layout.
A patch update from the proven 3.10.1 pin is unrelated and should be separate.
candidates.decision_rationale: The framework and Pages site already work; changing frameworks would not create the missing content.

candidates.name: @easyops-cn/docusaurus-search-local

evidence.claim: Repository search identified active Docusaurus v2/v3 offline search support and a 2026-05-31 push.
The npm registry reported current 0.55.2, matching the installed project version.

risks: Search completeness still needs a route/index assertion.
candidates.decision_rationale: It is already configured for /prompting and provides the required deterministic local search.

candidates.name: Ajv

evidence.claim: The npm registry reported 8.20.0, MIT, modified 2026-04-24.
Official documentation confirms support for JSON Schema 2020-12.

risks: JSON Schema cannot express source liveness, referenced skill existence, or producer/critic separation.
candidates.decision_rationale: Use a standard validator for shape and retain small project-owned semantic checks for cross-file invariants.

candidates.name: Linkinator

evidence.claim: The npm registry reported 7.6.1, MIT, Node >=20, modified 2026-02-27.

risks: Firecrawl returned no indexed official documentation result.
External link checks can fail for rate limits or transient vendor outages.
candidates.decision_rationale: Use it as a bounded built-site/external-link gate with retries and allowlists, not as the internal-link authority.

candidates.name: Playwright Test

evidence.claim: The npm registry reported 1.61.1, Apache-2.0, Node >=18, modified 2026-07-18.
Official documentation supports launching the documentation server as part of the test run.
Official projects support representative browser and viewport configurations.

risks: Browser installation adds CI time and cache requirements.
candidates.decision_rationale: It provides observable route, search, theme, responsive, and copy-button proof that a Markdown-only validator cannot.

candidates.name: @axe-core/playwright

evidence.claim: The npm registry reported 4.12.1, MPL-2.0, modified 2026-07-08.

risks: Firecrawl returned no indexed official GitHub documentation result.
Automated rules do not replace keyboard and visual accessibility review.
candidates.decision_rationale: Integrate with Playwright for repeatable WCAG checks on representative public routes.

candidates.name: Agent Skills specification

evidence.claim: The specification defines portable SKILL.md frontmatter and folder conventions used across supported harnesses.
The official guidance recommends compact core skills with detailed resources loaded progressively.

risks: The standard does not normalize harness-specific commands, permissions, plugins, or session semantics.
candidates.decision_rationale: Use it as the shared skill packaging contract while documenting every harness on its own terms.

candidates.name: orchestrate-prometheus-application project skill

evidence.claim: The existing template classifies all ten scenarios and is copied identically to six project harness directories.

risks: The current 270-word checklist does not generate or validate scenario/harness artifacts.
candidates.decision_rationale: Extend the existing portable router instead of creating a competing orchestration skill.

candidates.name: OpenAI Proxy repository

evidence.claim: Local source inspection found Axum OpenAI-compatible routes, AG-UI, optional A2A, MCP stdio/HTTP, ACP stdio, memory, Docker, and an OpenCode plugin.

risks: The repository contains no license declaration.
Generator lineage is operator-provided and must be labeled unless commit evidence establishes it.
Authentication and model catalog text can become stale.
candidates.decision_rationale: Use it as a source-inspected case study, not a dependency or unquestioned template.

candidates.name: remark-lint-frontmatter-schema

evidence.claim: Repository search found a focused frontmatter-to-JSON-Schema remark rule.
The npm registry reported the latest release was modified in 2023.

risks: Only validates frontmatter and does not cover cross-file prompt requirements.
candidates.decision_rationale: Ajv plus one project validator covers the broader contract with less plugin-specific surface.

candidates.name: Existing GitHub Pages Actions workflow

evidence.claim: GitHub supports custom Actions workflows for static-site generators.
The current pinned workflow builds, uploads, and deploys the Docusaurus artifact from main.

risks: The workflow currently has no recipe, source-currency, browser, accessibility, or route-completeness gates.
candidates.decision_rationale: Extend its build job with evidence gates; do not replace the working publication mechanism.

build_required.need: Ten complete Prometheus scenario recipe pages
build_required.why_no_candidate: The prompts encode project-specific architecture, skills, evidence, authority, and stop conditions that generic libraries cannot author or certify.
Each harness has different instruction discovery, skills, plugins, permissions, autonomous modes, and handoff semantics that require official-source synthesis.

build_required.need: Recipe, harness, and cross-file semantic validator
build_required.why_no_candidate: Ajv validates schema shape but cannot enforce non-empty staged prompts, existing skill references, producer/critic separation, architecture invariants, or public-boundary criteria.

build_required.need: Model claim evidence and routing generator
build_required.why_no_candidate: Exact model-source correspondence, dated availability, role vocabulary, confidence, and generated project routing are repository-specific policy.

build_required.need: Executable Feynman, KBD, Karpathy, PMPO, producer-critic, recovery, and retention examples
build_required.why_no_candidate: The installed Prometheus skills define the actual local commands and artifacts; the guide must synthesize and verify those exact contracts.
The case study requires source and commit-history analysis plus explicit separation of generated, current, verified, inferred, and unsupported behavior.

build_required.need: Representative Codex and ACP/CLI execution fixtures
build_required.why_no_candidate: Harness execution evidence depends on this repository's prompts, installed tools, authority limits, and retained artifacts.
